meta.title: TiDB Database Comparisons | PingCAP
meta.description: Honest, technically reviewed comparisons of TiDB against MySQL, PostgreSQL, CockroachDB, Aurora, YugabyteDB, and more. Find the right distributed SQL database.

props.headline: Find the Right Distributed SQL Database
props.subheadline: Honest, technically reviewed comparisons of TiDB against MySQL, PostgreSQL, CockroachDB, Aurora, YugabyteDB, and more. Written for engineers making architectural decisions.

props.title: TiDB vs. Leading Databases
props.subtitle: Detailed, workload-driven comparisons covering architecture, scalability, ACID behavior, HA, and TCO.

items.title: TiDB vs MySQL
items.description: Scale-out writes, HTAP, and distributed transactions vs. MySQL's single-primary simplicity. The definitive guide for teams hitting write limits or approaching sharding.

items.title: TiDB vs Amazon Aurora
items.description: Cloud-native distributed SQL vs. AWS's managed relational offering. Covers TCO at scale, multi-region HA, HTAP, and reducing AWS dependency.

items.title: TiDB vs CockroachDB
items.description: The two most-evaluated NewSQL databases. MySQL ecosystem vs. PostgreSQL wire protocol, HTAP capabilities, open-source licensing, and Kubernetes operations.

items.title: TiDB vs YugabyteDB
items.description: Two open-source distributed SQL databases with different protocol approaches. Compares HTAP support, MySQL vs. PostgreSQL compatibility, and operational complexity.

props.title: Workload-Driven Database Selection
props.subtitle: Not sure which database to shortlist? These guides cover the landscape by workload — not by vendor.

items.title: Best Distributed SQL Databases
items.description: A ranked guide to leading distributed SQL databases covering architecture, consistency guarantees, ecosystem fit, cloud options, and when each is the right call.

items.title: Best Database for AI Agents
items.description: Covers memory layers, vector search, ACID state management, and multi-agent scale. Compares TiDB, Pinecone, Redis, pgvector, Milvus, MongoDB Atlas, and more.

items.title: Best Vector Database for RAG
items.description: Retrieval quality, metadata filtering, hybrid search, indexing options, and production readiness. Updated quarterly to reflect real vendor capability changes.

items.title: Best Databases for SaaS at Scale
items.description: How to evaluate databases for multi-tenant SaaS — covering isolation models, horizontal scale, schema flexibility, cost predictability, and growth inflection points.

props.headline: About These Comparisons
props.subheadline: Each page is written with production context in mind — architecture tradeoffs, HA behavior under failure, migration complexity, and TCO at scale. Pages are reviewed by engineers who have run these systems in real workloads. Because PingCAP builds TiDB, we're explicit when a different database is the better fit. We update pages at least quarterly; the review date is shown on each page.

items.q: What is TiDB and how does it differ from MySQL?
items.a: TiDB is an open-source distributed SQL database that speaks the MySQL protocol. Unlike MySQL, TiDB scales horizontally across multiple nodes without sharding, supports distributed ACID transactions, and provides real-time analytics (HTAP) without ETL pipelines.

items.q: When should I choose TiDB over MySQL?
items.a: Choose TiDB when you need to scale writes across multiple nodes, eliminate sharding complexity, require HTAP capabilities, or need multi-region high availability. MySQL is simpler for single-node workloads and is the right choice if your data fits on one primary.

items.q: How does TiDB compare to PostgreSQL?
items.a: TiDB speaks MySQL protocol and is MySQL-compatible. PostgreSQL is more feature-rich for single-node OLTP. If you need distributed SQL with PostgreSQL wire protocol, evaluate YugabyteDB or CockroachDB.

items.q: Is TiDB open source? What license?
items.a: Yes, TiDB is open source under the Apache 2.0 license. TiDB Cloud (the managed service) offers both open-source and enterprise options with additional features.

items.q: How often are these comparisons updated?
items.a: All comparison pages are reviewed and updated quarterly. The review date is shown on each page. We track vendor changes, new features, and real-world production usage patterns.

items.q: Can I migrate from MySQL to TiDB without rewriting code?
items.a: Yes. TiDB is MySQL-compatible at the wire protocol level, so most MySQL applications connect without code changes. The migration process depends on your schema and workload complexity. See our migration guides for details.

props.title: Ready to Evaluate TiDB?
props.subtitle: Start a free trial on TiDB Cloud or deploy open source and run your workload today.
